text: Databases are essential for storing and organizing information. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases, like MySQL, store data in structured tables with rows and columns linked by relationships. You can retrieve data using queries, which are commands like SELECT or JOIN. Data modeling involves designing these tables and connections before building the database. Indexes are special structures that speed up queries by quickly locating data, similar to a book’s index. In contrast, NoSQL databases, like MongoDB, store data more flexibly, often as documents or key-value pairs, without fixed tables. This makes them suitable for unstructured data or rapid growth. Understanding these differences helps you choose the right database for your needs.
